reasoning: The prompt references "each resolution date listed below" but only provides the question close date of 2026-02-07. Assuming this date serves as the primary resolution point, we can apply normal climatology for the Alençon station. The historical mean daily temperature in early November (Nov 9) is approximately 8.5-9.0°C. In contrast, the average temperature around early February (Feb 7) typically drops to about 5.5-6.0°C. By treating the temperatures as approximately independent normal distributions, the expected difference (Feb 7 minus Nov 9) is roughly -3.3°C. Considering the standard deviations of winter/autumn daily temperatures in this region are approximately 3.5°C and 4.0°C respectively, the combined standard deviation of the difference is about 5.3°C. Calculating the probability that the difference is above zero yields a Z-score of roughly 0.62, strictly reflecting an approximate 26% probability that early February will be warmer than early November.
